name: DevCycle Feature Flag API
what_it_does: Feature flagging platform with REST API and official OpenFeature provider — offering edge-evaluated feature flags with zero-latency evaluation, multi-variate experiments, and GitOps-friendly configuration, with strong OpenFeature standard compatibility.
use_cases: Feature flag evaluation at edge for zero-latency decisions in high-performance apps
OpenFeature-compatible feature flag management for standardized SDK integration
Managing feature flags via GitOps workflows with code-managed flag configuration
A/B testing and multi-variate experiments with statistical analysis via API
Gradual rollouts with targeting rules managed programmatically via REST API
not_for: Teams needing data warehouse-native experimentation (use Eppo)
Simple on/off feature toggles where LaunchDarkly's free tier is sufficient
Organizations needing enterprise analytics included in flag platform
best_when: A developer team needs OpenFeature-standard feature flags with edge evaluation, GitOps-friendly management, and clean API for programmatic flag control.
avoid_when: You need advanced statistical experimentation analysis or data warehouse integration for experiment results.

auth.notes: SDK key (client vs server) for flag evaluation. Management API uses OAuth2-style token. Separate client/server SDK keys for security. Project and environment-scoped access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited flags, 1,000 monthly active users
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth from $50/month; Business from $250; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for individual developers. Acquired by Taplytics team with deep experimentation expertise. Good OpenFeature adoption means investment in standards compliance. Competitive pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Local SDK evaluation — no network latency per flag call; API has rate limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenFeature compatibility means standardized SDK interface across providers. Management API comprehensive for flag CRUD. Edge evaluation via SDK means no per-evaluation API calls. Good documentation with code examples.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Flag evaluation is deterministic per user context and flag version. Management API operations are idempotent for same parameters. Flag configuration updates propagate to edge — eventual consistency.
known_agent_gotchas: SDK configuration fetched periodically — flag changes have propagation delay (seconds)
Client vs server SDK keys serve different purposes — use server key for backend agents
OpenFeature provider means vendor-neutral SDK — but DevCycle-specific features use native SDK
Edge evaluation requires SDK initialization — management REST API for configuration only

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Client/server key separation prevents client-side key abuse. SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant. US and EU data residency. OpenFeature standard compliance improves vendor-neutral security posture.
